findings.title: Reset-token digest uses non-constant-time equality comparison
Reset tokens are verified without being consumed, permitting reuse until expiry
findings.quote: if (!record || record.used || record.expiresAt < Date.now()) return false;

reasoning: bcrypt cost 12 is used for passwords. Reset tokens use CSPRNG generation, but their digest comparison is not constant-time and successful verification does not mark the token as used.
